name: GRT Agent Chain with 7-Layer Truth Validation
author: Swapna Ketavarapu
description: A governed chain of AI agents that produces a verified GRD and working governed system. Each agent has its own GRT. Versions are frozen and validated against the 7 Layers of Truth. A self-improvement loop governed by an Evolution Agent updates prompts across versions.

emergency_circuit_breaker.description: Immediate halt mechanism that bypasses the version lifecycle when active harm is detected. Separate from normal threshold escalation — this is the fire alarm, not the thermostat.
trigger_conditions: Reality Feedback Agent detects harm occurring NOW (not predicted — actual)
Any authority role manually triggers emergency stop
System output directly contradicts a Meta-layer moral constraint
Protected-attribute discrimination detected in live production decisions
actions_on_trigger: IMMEDIATELY stop the governed system from producing new outputs
Notify ALL authority roles simultaneously (not escalation chain — broadcast)
Log the trigger condition with full context for post-mortem
Require explicit release from deployment_owner + ethical_escalation to resume
emergency_circuit_breaker.release_requires: Joint approval from deployment_owner AND ethical_escalation with documented resolution

rework_protocol.description: Formalized backward loops when a downstream agent discovers a problem requiring upstream rework. The chain doesn't just block — it routes back with a structured request.

schema.requesting_agent: number (agent ID that found the problem)
schema.target_agent: number (agent ID that must redo work)
schema.required_change: string (what the target agent must produce differently)
schema.evidence: string (the data/finding that proves the rework is needed)
schema.escalation_if_unresolved: After 3 rework iterations without resolution → escalate to Truth Conflict Resolver (Agent 17)

allowed_backward_routes.reason: Validator finds rule gap → Rule Derivation adds new rule
Validator finds threshold contradiction → Threshold Calibrator redesigns
Validator finds goal incoherence → Goal Architect revises
Testing Agent finds untestable case → Test Case Author rewrites
Testing Agent finds connector violation → Connecting Agent fixes
Testing Agent finds threshold miscalibrated → Threshold Calibrator recalibrates
Truth Conflict Resolver finds goal must change → Goal Architect revises
Reality Feedback diverges → Evolution Agent must propose fix

chain_cost_model.description: Tracks cumulative governance cost per run. Prevents governance from becoming heavier than the thing it governs.

tracked_metrics.description: Total LLM tokens consumed across all agents in one chain run
Total time humans spend reviewing escalations, approving weights, resolving conflicts
Wall-clock time from chain start to deployment gate
Direct cost: LLM API + human labor at loaded rate

thresholds.condition: Governance cost exceeds 15% of governed system's first-year value
thresholds.action: Flag for simplification — governance is too heavy

thresholds.condition: Human review time exceeds 40 hours per run
Calendar time exceeds 2 weeks for a single GRD production

thresholds.action: Structural problem — escalate to Evolution Agent for chain redesign

chain_cost_model.cost_benefit_ratio: Tracked per run. If ratio worsens for >3 consecutive runs → Evolution Agent must address.

seven_layers_of_truth.progression: Quantum > Physical > Biological > Cognitive > Cultural > Civilizational > Meta

layers.name: Quantum
layers.freeze_gate: Does the version leave room for evolution? Temporal expiry set? Composability hooks defined?

layers.name: Physical
layers.freeze_gate: Tests pass? Connectors healthy? Data flowing? Within budget?

layers.name: Biological
layers.freeze_gate: Fire rate <40%? Override flow ≤3 clicks? Human capacity respected?

layers.name: Cognitive
layers.freeze_gate: No contradictions? Validator passed? Assumptions testable?

layers.name: Cultural
layers.freeze_gate: Cultural assumptions declared? Frame origin culturally situated? Language inclusive?

layers.name: Civilizational
layers.freeze_gate: Regulatory aligned? Authority routing maps to real roles? Composable?

layers.name: Meta
layers.freeze_gate: Serves moral purpose? Incentive mapping honest? Makes things better?

agents.name: Incentive Auditor
agents.goal: Map incentive structure — who benefits, who pays, where biases accumulate
rules: Must produce quantitative metrics not just qualitative assertions

detection_metrics.self_score_vs_acceptance_ratio: For each agent: ratio of self-assessed confidence to downstream agent acceptance rate. Healthy = close to 1.0. >1.5 = overconfident. <0.5 = underconfident.
detection_metrics.human_override_pattern: Track which agents get consistently overridden by humans and in which direction (stricter or more lenient). Systematic one-direction override = incentive pressure.
detection_metrics.weight_drift_detection: Track whether the same authority role always adjusts weights in the same direction across sessions. Consistent downward pressure on honest_reporting = systematic gaming.
detection_metrics.cost_bearer_visibility: For each rule and threshold: who actually bears the cost when it fires? Is it the same stakeholder every time? If one stakeholder bears >60% of governance cost → flag inequity.

agents.name: Domain Analyst
agents.goal: Extract decision space, stakeholders, data, risks. Classify truth layer.

agents.name: Goal Architect
agents.goal: Draft Goal with 6 criteria (frame, residue, incentive, non-goals, temporal, composability)

agents.name: Threshold Calibrator
agents.goal: Define 3-7 thresholds with fire-rate bands and authority routing

agents.name: Residue & HCI Analyst
agents.goal: Map residue domains, reason weights, deployment level, expected HCI

agents.name: Validator / Adversary
agents.goal: Find gaps and weaknesses. Test against adversarial scenarios.

agents.name: Finalizer
agents.goal: Produce valid GRD JSON incorporating validation feedback

agents.name: UX Architect
agents.goal: Design UI making governance visible and actionable
rules: Must produce a GRD-to-UI traceability matrix showing which UI element enforces which GRD rule
Traceability matrix must be RE-VERIFIED by Validator (Agent 6) after any Evolution Agent change that touches rules or thresholds — not just on initial build
thresholds: GRD rule with no traceability to a UI element → BLOCK build
Post-evolution traceability gap detected → rework back to UX Architect
agents.enforcement_verification: After UX design AND after any evolution-driven rule/threshold change, the Validator (Agent 6) must re-verify the traceability matrix. If evolution breaks traceability, UX Architect must update before new version freezes.

agents.name: Connector Identifier
agents.goal: Map all integration points required by GRD
rules: Must produce a connector health specification with SLA for each integration
thresholds: No SLA defined for a governance-critical connector → block

agents.name: Connecting Agent
agents.goal: Build connectors enforcing authority routing and data contracts
rules: Must implement GRD-rule-to-code traceability (each connector method references the GRD rule it enforces)
Must implement silent-failure detection: if a governance event (threshold fire, override, residue disclosure) fails to propagate, raise an alarm within 30 seconds
thresholds: Governance event not confirmed within 30s → HALT that transaction path
agents.build_verification: Agent 12 (Testing) must run governance-specific integration tests on every connector before Phase 3 can complete.

agents.name: Testing Agent
agents.goal: Run tests, report honestly, map failures to GRD violations

agents.name: Deployment Gate
rules: No deploy if critical test fails
No deploy if TBD in roster

agents.name: Version Controller
agents.goal: Freeze versions, track lineage, gate releases

agents.name: Evolution Agent
agents.goal: Write improved prompts/configs from Monitor findings

agents.name: Truth Conflict Resolver
agents.goal: Resolve contradictions between truth layers when they produce conflicting requirements
rules: Never optimize one layer while silently violating another
Every resolution must document the tradeoff explicitly
Every resolution must name the authority owner who approved it
Never resolve a Meta vs Civilizational conflict without governance review
Never resolve by eliminating a layer — all layers have standing

DEFAULT PRESUMPTION: When Meta and Civilizational conflict, presume toward preserving Meta (moral purpose) unless deployment_owner + ethical_escalation jointly approve otherwise with documented public rationale
thresholds: >2 unresolved layer conflicts in same GRD → human escalation
Meta vs Civilizational conflict → mandatory governance board review
Resolution that degrades any layer metric >20% → block until authority approves
Same conflict recurs across >3 versions → structural redesign needed

agents.name: Reality Feedback Agent
agents.goal: Compare predicted outcomes with actual outcomes. Feed environmental reality back into the chain.
rules: Must measure prediction accuracy at declared cadence
Never ignore outcome data that contradicts the GRD's assumptions
Must distinguish system-caused outcomes from environmental changes
Never attribute external events to system behavior without evidence
Must have access to EXTERNAL telemetry (market data, user behavior, environmental signals) — not just internal system logs
Never attribute success to the system when external tailwinds dominate — if market conditions improved independently, the system did not cause the improvement
thresholds: Prediction error >20% on any GRD-declared metric → trigger evolution review
Unexpected consequence not covered by any rule → flag for Rule Derivation (Agent 3)
Residue domain impact higher than weight predicted → recalibrate reason weights
>3 consecutive measurement periods with degrading accuracy → HALT and reassess
System claims improvement but external indicators show market-wide improvement → flag false attribution

human_authority_map.description: Formalized authority routing for all governance decisions. Every escalation, override, and deployment decision maps to a named role.

threshold_owner.authority: Approve or override individual threshold-triggered escalations
examples: T1 fires → category buyer reviews data vintage
T3 fires → finance director reviews high-value contract

override_owner.authority: Override any recommendation when buyer judgment supersedes model output
examples: VP Procurement overrides supplier ranking for strategic reasons

ethical_escalation.authority: Resolve Meta vs Civilizational conflicts. Approve goal-level changes. Review incentive mapping.

deployment_owner.authority: Final go/no-go on deployment. Owns the 7-layer freeze ceremony sign-off.
examples: Approves v1.0 freeze after all layers pass

evolution_approver.authority: Approve major version changes (goal changes, agent restructuring). Gate for Evolution Agent proposals.

reality_reviewer.authority: Validate Reality Feedback Agent findings. Confirm whether outcome divergence is system-caused or environmental.
examples: Supplier failure increase — is it our model or market conditions?

weighted_goals.description: Every agent's goal is decomposed into weighted sub-objectives. Authorized human contributors can adjust weights through the Authority Dashboard UI. Weight changes are versioned and require the role specified in permission_level.

schema_per_agent.constraint: All weights must sum to 1.0 within each agent. The UI enforces this via normalization.

goal_weights.description: Declare what the model cannot see
Map who pays and who benefits
Specify what must not be optimized
Map every failure to a specific GRD violation
Ensure all rules and thresholds are tested

weight_change_rules: Only the role specified in permission_level can change that sub-goal's weight
All weight changes are logged with reason, timestamp, and identity
Weights must sum to 1.0 — changing one redistributes across others (UI handles normalization)
A weight change >0.15 in a single adjustment requires evolution_approver approval
Weight changes take effect on the NEXT run — never mid-execution
Weight changes to a frozen version create a new patch version (x.x.+1)
WEIGHT FLOOR: No sub-goal weight can drop below 0.05 regardless of authority. This prevents slow walk-down attacks where multiple small reductions effectively zero out a critical sub-goal.
CUMULATIVE DRIFT PROTECTION: If a sub-goal's weight has been reduced >3 times in the same direction without increase, flag for Incentive Auditor review
CRITICAL SUB-GOALS: Sub-goals tagged 'integrity_critical' (e.g., honest_reporting on Agent 12) have a floor of 0.15, not 0.05. These cannot be reduced below 15% of the agent's attention.

authority_dashboard_ui.description: A web UI where authorized human contributors view and adjust agent goal weights, review escalations, approve version changes, and monitor the chain.

access_control.authentication: Role-based login. Each user maps to one or more authority_map roles.
access_control.authorization: Users can only modify weights where their role matches the sub-goal's permission_level.
access_control.audit_trail: Every action is logged: who, what, when, why.

panels.name: Agent Overview
panels.description: See all 19 agents, their current phase, status (idle/running/blocked/completed), and performance summary.

panels.name: Weight Tuning
panels.description: For each agent, see sub-goals as sliders. Only sliders the user's role permits are editable. Others are read-only.
actions: Preview impact (what would change in next run)

panels.name: Escalation Queue
panels.description: Pending escalations from agents that fired thresholds requiring human input.
actions: Route to different authority if needed

panels.name: Truth Conflict Queue
panels.description: Active truth layer conflicts from Agent 17 requiring resolution.

panels.name: Version Control
panels.description: View frozen versions, pending proposals from Evolution Agent, A/B test results.

panels.name: Reality Dashboard
panels.description: Agent 18's findings — predicted vs actual outcomes over time.

panels.name: 7-Layer Validation Status
panels.description: Current freeze validation state. Shows which layers pass/fail for the active version.

authority_dashboard_ui.technology: Self-contained HTML/JS (like existing dashboards) OR React app with auth backend — TBD based on deployment needs

root_grd.goal: Given a domain/problem, produce a verified GRD + governed system + tested deployment. All agents governed. All versions truth-validated.
non_goals: Full autonomy without human authority owners — this chain never operates without named humans at decision points
Replacing human judgment — the chain augments and structures human governance, never substitutes it
Universal applicability without adaptation — each domain requires cultural/contextual calibration
residue_declaration: Cultural and identity layer mismatches that emerge only in live multi-stakeholder use — no synthetic run can surface these fully
Power dynamics between authority roles that influence weight adjustments — the system tracks drift but cannot perceive coercion
Emergent agent interaction effects that only manifest at scale across many parallel runs

temporal_expiry.spec_version_expiry: This spec expires 2027-04-22 (12 months from authoring) or upon any structural change to the 7-layer model, whichever comes first
temporal_expiry.review_trigger: Must be re-validated if deployed in >3 new domains, or if Evolution Agent proposes >10 cumulative changes
temporal_expiry.renewal_authority: evolution_approver + ethical_escalation must jointly approve renewal or revision

rules: Never skip human review at phase transitions
Never allow agent self-evaluation without external check
Version lineage must remain human-readable and auditable for at least 7 years (Civilizational layer requirement)
If total agents in chain exceed 25, force consolidation review — agent proliferation is a governance risk
thresholds: >3 agents escalate in same run → halt
7-layer validation fails → block freeze and return to responsible agent
Governance cost >30% of governed system value for >2 consecutive runs → emergency review (not just flag — auto-escalate to deployment_owner + ethical_escalation)
